meta.title: Chairside reference — emergency cards + bench cheat-sheet
meta.version: v1.0 (from CARD-A v2.0, CARD-V v2.1, CARD-VC v2.0, QUICK-REFERENCE-SHEET-01 v1.3)
meta.note: Faithful transcription of the licensed laminated chairside cards for in-app read-only reference. The treating provider owns the clinical decision; this is decision-support, not a prescription. References MASTER-MENU-01 v3.3.

CARD_A.title: Anaphylaxis — Chairside Emergency
recognize: Sense of impending doom, sudden anxiety
CARD_A.act_first: EPINEPHRINE 0.3 mg IM lateral thigh (1:1,000 · adult) IMMEDIATELY on recognition — before diphenhydramine, before steroids, before calling for help if alone. Repeat q5–15 min ×2 PRN if symptoms persist.
concurrent: Stop infusion immediately. Keep IV catheter in place and patent (KVO with NS).
Position supine, legs elevated (sit upright if airway compromised).
O2 non-rebreather 10–15 L/min. Apply SpO2 + BP cuff; monitor continuously.
Note time of EPI dose and each subsequent action.
CARD_A.call_ems: CALL 911 · ACTIVATE EMS · DO NOT DELAY
secondary_meds: Diphenhydramine 25–50 mg IV slow push or IM (H1)
Methylprednisolone (Solu-Medrol) 40–125 mg IV slow push or IM — reduces biphasic-reaction risk
NS 250–500 mL bolus if hypotensive (BP < 90/60 or symptomatic)
Ondansetron 4 mg IV/IM if persistent nausea/vomiting (optional)
post_event: FORM-04 Adverse Event Incident Report within 24 h.
Observe ≥ 4 h after resolution — biphasic risk peaks 4–12 h post-event.
Discharge only with EMS or ED transport; never self-drive after EPI.

CARD_V.title: Vasovagal Response — Chairside
act: STOP infusion by closing the roller clamp. Maintain IV access if safe/patent.
Position supine in brief / modified Trendelenburg as tolerated; legs elevated.

Do NOT use Trendelenburg if: vomiting · aspiration risk · respiratory distress · SpO2 < 94% · chest pain · suspected anaphylaxis · seizure · focal neuro · head/neck trauma · pregnancy concern — use supine flat, legs mildly elevated.
Recheck BP·HR·RR·SpO2·LOC. Cold compress forehead/neck. Reassure. Oral fluids only when fully alert.
Document on FORM-05 SOAP; FORM-04 for syncope/LOC/EMS/referral/deviation.
escalate_911: LOC > 1 min, repeated LOC, or incomplete recovery
Persistent SBP < 90 or HR < 50 after 5 min supine + legs elevated
Chest pain · dyspnea · respiratory distress · cyanosis · SpO2 not correcting
Focal neuro — weakness · slurred speech · facial droop · seizure · prolonged confusion
Head/body trauma from fall · suspected fracture
Altered mental status · concern for non-vasovagal (cardiac/neuro/metabolic) cause
CARD_V.vs_anaphylaxis: Vasovagal: pale/cool/clammy, clear airway, bradycardia, improves with legs-up. Anaphylaxis: urticaria/flushing/angioedema, wheeze/stridor, usually tachycardia, does NOT improve — needs Epinephrine → CARD-A.
prevent: Pre-hydrate; light meal 1–2 h before (avoid fasting)
CARD_V.post_event: Do not restart same session unless uncomplicated vasovagal, fully resolved, normal vitals ≥ 15 min. Remain in clinic until recovered; escort home; no driving until cleared; 24-h follow-up call; notify MD for QA within 24 h (same-day if serious).

CARD_VC.title: Vitamin C Verbal Screen (doses 6.1–10 g)

when.action: No formal verbal screen (standard intake applies)
Run the full 9-question screen + document each response in chart / FORM-05
Not within wellness scope — refer to standard medical evaluation

nine_questions.if_yes: Cap Vit C at 2 g or decline

nine_questions.q: Ever had a reaction to high-dose Vit C?
Mediterranean, Sephardic Jewish, African, or SE Asian ancestry AND family history of anemia?
End-stage renal disease or on dialysis?
Diabetic AND use a point-of-care finger-stick glucometer?

CARD_VC.citations: Padayatty SJ et al. (2010) PLoS ONE; AANP IV Nutrient Therapy Guidelines.

emergency_cart: Ondansetron 4 mg/2 mL ×2 · 0.9% NS 500 mL ×2 · IV tubing
O2 tank · regulator · non-rebreather mask · nasal cannula
Ambu bag (BVM) · oral airways · AED (charged, pads in date, daily self-test)
Pulse oximeter · BP cuff (manual+auto) · stethoscope · glucometer + strips · sharps
Cold compress/ice · sterile gauze · alcohol wipes · gloves · oral electrolyte solution
Incident report forms on cart · phone with 911 · emergency contact list posted

quick_reference.purpose: Single-page bench reference for the supervised ARNP during compounding & order review — route, standard dose, wellness ceiling, key gate per ingredient.
quick_reference.defaults: Catheter 22G · base 500 mL 0.9% NS (1 L only for HYDR-01) · Methylcobalamin only (no Cyano/Hydroxo) · ready-to-use only (no lyophilized) · preservative-free Vit C / Glutathione / Calcium Gluconate · no ALA in packet standard · no Sodium Bicarbonate in packet.

tiers.green: Routine wellness, no extra gate beyond standard intake.
tiers.yellow: Additional gate required (verbal screen · IM-only · special handling · lab warning).

base_fluids.name: Lactated Ringer's 1 L
base_fluids.note: HYDR-01 alternate; NOT with high-dose Vit C

ingredients.name: Calcium Gluconate PF
ingredients.gate: NOT with Vit C in same bag

ingredients.name: NAD+ (ready-to-use)
ingredients.gate: 250 mg = 2 h first / 90–120 min subsequent · 500 mg = 3–4 h · not mixed

ingredients.name: Trace Mineral Injection (Zn+Cu+Mn+Se)
ingredients.gate: IV-11; not stacked w/ standalone Zn/Se

prn_meds.name: Diphenhydramine (Benadryl)
prn_meds.warning: Sedation · anticholinergic · not a wellness add-on

prn_meds.name: Ondansetron (Zofran)
prn_meds.warning: QT-prolongation risk · not a casual add-on

prn_meds.name: Epinephrine 1:1,000
prn_meds.warning: Activate EMS/911 · first-line in anaphylaxis · NOT a wellness ingredient

prn_meds.name: Methylprednisolone (Solu-Medrol)
prn_meds.warning: After EPI only · onset delayed 4–6 h · prevents biphasic reaction

prn_meds.name: Oxygen (O2)
prn_meds.warning: Mandatory chairside equipment · verify tank daily
